_comment: Agent-to-agent handoff example: research agent → writing agent

blocks.content: Write a 1200-word technical blog post about Model Context Protocol (MCP). Audience: senior engineers. Tone: authoritative but accessible. Include: what MCP is, why it matters, a simple code example, and adoption tips. Do not include marketing fluff.

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard by Anthropic (Nov 2024) that lets AI models securely connect to external data sources and tools via a client-server architecture. Clients are LLM apps; servers expose tools and resources. Transport: stdio, HTTP+SSE. Authentication: OAuth 2.0. Key primitives: Resources (data), Tools (functions), Prompts (templates).

As of Q1 2026: 500+ community MCP servers on GitHub. Major integrations: Cursor, Zed, Claude Desktop, Continue.dev. Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and OpenAI all shipping MCP-compatible clients. Enterprise adoption accelerating in financial services and healthcare verticals.

# Minimal MCP server in Python
from mcp.server import Server
from mcp.types import Tool, TextContent

server = Server('my-tools')

@server.list_tools()
async def list_tools():
    return [Tool(name='get_weather', description='Get current weather', inputSchema={...})]

@server.call_tool()
async def call_tool(name: str, arguments: dict):
    if name == 'get_weather':
        return [TextContent(type='text', text=f"Weather for {arguments['city']}: Sunny, 72°F")]

if __name__ == '__main__':
    import asyncio
    asyncio.run(server.run())

Key angle: MCP solves the N×M integration problem. Each AI app no longer needs custom connectors for every tool. Pain point for engineers: tool sprawl. Competitor context: LangChain's tool ecosystem is fragmented; MCP offers standardization.
HN thread 2025-03-01: Top comments positive about standardization. Main concerns: server security, auth complexity. Upvote ratio 94%. 340 comments.
